text.title: Speech-to-Text Rules
text.summary: Language parameter, file size limits, audio preprocessing, caching, and production model selection rules.

section.title: API Usage Rules

rule.statement: Always specify the language parameter explicitly in Whisper calls. Never rely on auto-detection in production.
rule.rationale: Auto-detection adds ~200ms latency and occasionally misidentifies similar languages (Ukrainian/Russian, Serbian/Croatian). Explicit language="uk" eliminates both problems.

rule.statement: Split audio files into chunks under 24MB before uploading to the Whisper API. Use pydub with 10-minute chunks exported as MP3 at 64kbps.
rule.rationale: Whisper API rejects files over 25MB. A 90-minute meeting WAV typically exceeds this. Chunking at 10 minutes leaves margin for encoding overhead.

section.reference: pydub helper to split audio into chunks under 24MB

example.code:
from pydub import AudioSegment import math, os
def split_audio(path: str, chunk_min: int = 10, out_dir: str = "/tmp") -> list[str]: audio = AudioSegment.from_file(path) chunk_ms = chunk_min * 60 * 1000 n = math.ceil(len(audio) / chunk_ms) paths = [] for i in range(n): chunk = audio[i * chunk_ms:(i + 1) * chunk_ms] p = os.path.join(out_dir, f"chunk_{i:03d}.mp3") chunk.export(p, format="mp3", bitrate="64k") paths.append(p) return paths

rule.statement: Pre-process audio before transcription: convert to 16kHz mono WAV, normalize amplitude, apply noise reduction. Improves accuracy 5-15%.
rule.rationale: Whisper was trained on varied audio but performs best on clean 16kHz mono. Format conversion with ffmpeg before API upload is a low-cost accuracy improvement.

rule.statement: Cache transcriptions by SHA-256 hash of the audio file bytes. Never re-transcribe the same file.
rule.rationale: Re-transcribing the same file wastes API cost and time. Content-based hashing catches identical files with different names.

rule.statement: Use the Whisper prompt parameter to include domain-specific terms, speaker names, and expected vocabulary — keep it under 100 tokens.
rule.rationale: The prompt parameter is prepended to model context (not searched against audio), reducing substitution errors for domain terms. Over 100 tokens dilutes the effect.

rule.statement: Use Deepgram or AssemblyAI WebSocket streaming for real-time requirements below 300ms. Use faster-whisper locally for high-volume (over 10k hours/month).
rule.rationale: Whisper API is batch-only — it requires a complete file upload. Streaming STT providers process audio frames as they arrive. Local faster-whisper is significantly cheaper at scale.

antipattern.description: Polling AssemblyAI or AWS Transcribe with a synchronous await.
antipattern.reason: Long audio transcription jobs via cloud services are async — agent must poll a status URL. Synchronous waiting blocks the event loop for minutes.
